name: Testosterone Cypionate
what_it_is: A long-acting esterified form of testosterone suspended in oil. It is the most commonly prescribed injectable testosterone in the United States.
common_use_context: Used in hormone replacement therapy for men with primary or hypogonadotropic hypogonadism.
mechanism_brief: After intramuscular or subcutaneous injection, the cypionate ester is gradually cleaved by serum esterases, releasing free testosterone which binds to androgen receptors.
fda_approved_for: Replacement therapy in males with conditions associated with deficiency or absence of endogenous testosterone (primary and hypogonadotropic hypogonadism).

name: Testosterone Enanthate
what_it_is: A long-acting esterified form of testosterone in oil suspension, structurally similar to cypionate with one less carbon in the ester chain.
common_use_context: Used in hormone replacement therapy for men with hypogonadism; also approved as an autoinjector formulation (Xyosted).
mechanism_brief: The enanthate ester is hydrolyzed by serum esterases following injection, releasing testosterone that activates androgen receptors in target tissues.
fda_approved_for: Replacement therapy in conditions associated with a deficiency or absence of endogenous testosterone in males.

name: Testosterone Propionate
what_it_is: A short-acting esterified form of testosterone in oil. The propionate ester is the shortest of the common testosterone esters.
common_use_context: Used in hormone replacement therapy, though far less commonly than cypionate or enanthate due to its shorter duration.
mechanism_brief: Cleaved rapidly by serum esterases after injection, releasing free testosterone which binds androgen receptors. Shorter ester chain produces faster release and clearance.
fda_approved_for: Replacement therapy in males with deficiency of endogenous testosterone (limited commercial availability in the US).

name: Testosterone Undecanoate
what_it_is: A very long-acting esterified form of testosterone available as an injectable in castor oil (Aveed) or an oral softgel (Jatenzo).
common_use_context: Used in hormone replacement therapy where extended dosing intervals are desired.
mechanism_brief: The long aliphatic undecanoate ester slows hydrolysis, prolonging release. Oral undecanoate is absorbed via the intestinal lymphatic system, bypassing first-pass hepatic metabolism.
fda_approved_for: Replacement therapy in adult males for conditions associated with a deficiency or absence of endogenous testosterone.

name: Human Chorionic Gonadotropin
what_it_is: A glycoprotein hormone naturally produced during pregnancy. Pharmaceutical HCG is supplied as a lyophilized powder for reconstitution.
common_use_context: Used in hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, induction of ovulation, and cryptorchidism. In men on TRT it is sometimes used adjunctively to maintain testicular function.
mechanism_brief: HCG mimics luteinizing hormone (LH) by binding to LH receptors on Leydig cells in the testes, stimulating intratesticular testosterone production.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder; reconstituted with bacteriostatic or sterile water prior to injection.
fda_approved_for: Hypogonadotropic hypogonadism in males, prepubertal cryptorchidism, and induction of ovulation in females.

name: Anastrozole
what_it_is: A non-steroidal aromatase inhibitor available as an oral tablet.
common_use_context: Used in postmenopausal women with hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer. Off-label, sometimes used by clinicians to manage elevated estradiol in men on testosterone therapy.
mechanism_brief: Reversibly binds the aromatase enzyme, blocking the peripheral conversion of androgens to estrogens and lowering circulating estradiol.
fda_approved_for: Adjuvant treatment and first/second-line treatment of hormone receptor-positive breast cancer in postmenopausal women.

name: Enclomiphene
what_it_is: The pure trans-isomer of clomiphene, a selective estrogen receptor modulator (SERM).
common_use_context: Investigated as a treatment for secondary hypogonadism in men who wish to preserve fertility. Frequently prescribed via compounding pharmacies.
mechanism_brief: Antagonizes estrogen receptors at the hypothalamus and pituitary, disinhibiting GnRH release and thereby increasing LH, FSH, and endogenous testosterone production.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved as a standalone drug; available through compounding pharmacies. Prior NDA was withdrawn.

name: Tamoxifen
what_it_is: A selective estrogen receptor modulator (SERM) available as oral tablets or oral solution.
common_use_context: Used in treatment and prevention of hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer. Off-label uses include gynecomastia and hypogonadism management.
mechanism_brief: Acts as a competitive estrogen receptor antagonist in breast tissue while exhibiting partial agonist activity in other tissues such as bone and endometrium.
fda_approved_for: Treatment of metastatic breast cancer, adjuvant treatment of breast cancer, and reduction in breast cancer incidence in high-risk women.

name: Clomiphene
what_it_is: A selective estrogen receptor modulator (SERM) consisting of a mixture of two isomers (zuclomiphene and enclomiphene).
common_use_context: Used for ovulation induction in anovulatory women. Off-label, used in men for secondary hypogonadism, particularly when fertility preservation is desired.
mechanism_brief: Blocks estrogen receptor feedback at the hypothalamus and pituitary, increasing pulsatile GnRH and downstream gonadotropin release.
fda_approved_for: Treatment of ovulatory dysfunction in women desiring pregnancy.

name: Semaglutide
what_it_is: A long-acting glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist available as a once-weekly subcutaneous injection or once-daily oral tablet.
common_use_context: Used in type 2 diabetes for glycemic control (Ozempic, Rybelsus) and in chronic weight management (Wegovy).
mechanism_brief: Activates GLP-1 receptors, augmenting glucose-dependent insulin secretion, suppressing glucagon release, slowing gastric emptying, and reducing appetite via central pathways.
fda_approved_for: Type 2 diabetes mellitus (Ozempic, Rybelsus); chronic weight management in adults with obesity or overweight with comorbidity (Wegovy); cardiovascular risk reduction in type 2 diabetes.

name: Tirzepatide
what_it_is: A long-acting dual GIP and GLP-1 receptor agonist administered as a once-weekly subcutaneous injection.
common_use_context: Used in type 2 diabetes (Mounjaro), chronic weight management (Zepbound), and moderate-to-severe obstructive sleep apnea with obesity (Zepbound).
mechanism_brief: Simultaneously activates both glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide (GIP) and GLP-1 receptors, enhancing insulin secretion, suppressing glucagon, slowing gastric emptying, and reducing food intake.
fda_approved_for: Type 2 diabetes (Mounjaro); chronic weight management and obstructive sleep apnea in adults with obesity (Zepbound).

name: Retatrutide
what_it_is: An investigational triple agonist of GIP, GLP-1, and glucagon receptors under clinical development as a once-weekly subcutaneous injection.
common_use_context: Under investigation for obesity and type 2 diabetes in clinical trials.
mechanism_brief: Triple agonism of GIP, GLP-1, and glucagon receptors, combining incretin-mediated effects on insulin and satiety with glucagon-mediated effects on energy expenditure.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; investigational compound in late-stage clinical trials.

name: Liraglutide
what_it_is: A once-daily GLP-1 receptor agonist administered by subcutaneous injection.
common_use_context: Used in type 2 diabetes (Victoza) and chronic weight management (Saxenda).
mechanism_brief: Activates GLP-1 receptors, augmenting glucose-dependent insulin release, suppressing glucagon, slowing gastric emptying, and reducing appetite.
fda_approved_for: Type 2 diabetes (Victoza, adults and pediatric patients aged 10+); chronic weight management (Saxenda).

name: Cagrilintide
what_it_is: An investigational long-acting amylin analog under clinical development, often studied as a fixed-dose combination with semaglutide (CagriSema).
common_use_context: Under investigation for chronic weight management in clinical trials.
mechanism_brief: Acts as a non-selective amylin receptor agonist, reducing food intake by activating amylin and calcitonin receptors in central nervous system regions involved in satiety.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; investigational compound.

name: BPC-157
typical_storage: Lyophilized vials stable at room temperature; reconstituted solution refrigerated at 2-8°C and used within ~30 days
what_it_is: A synthetic pentadecapeptide (15 amino acids) derived from a partial sequence of a protective protein found in human gastric juice. Supplied as a lyophilized powder; sometimes sold as the stabilized arginate salt 'BPC-157 arginate' which has improved aqueous stability.
common_use_context: Most widely studied in rodent models of tendon, ligament, muscle, gut, and brain injury. ROUTES: most commonly self-administered subcutaneous injection near the area of interest (250-500 mcg, 1-2x/day). Intramuscular injection is also reported. Oral dosing has been used in GI-focused protocols — bioavailability is debated, but its activity in the gut lumen plausibly explains anecdotal benefit there. Topical/transdermal formulations exist but are poorly characterized.
mechanism_brief: Proposed mechanisms include modulation of nitric oxide signaling, upregulation of growth factors (VEGF, FGF, EGF), promotion of angiogenesis, and improved expression of tendon/ligament-relevant receptors. Effects on the dopamine and serotonin systems have also been reported in animal models. Human pharmacokinetics are not well characterized.
reconstitution_note: Lyophilized powder reconstitutes with bacteriostatic water (commonly 2-3 mL per 5 mg vial → 250 mcg per 0.15 mL on a 0.5 mL insulin syringe at U-100). Swirl, do not shake. Refrigerate after reconstitution.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: TB-500
what_it_is: A synthetic peptide fragment related to thymosin beta-4, a naturally occurring 43-amino-acid actin-binding protein. Supplied as a lyophilized powder.
common_use_context: Studied in preclinical research for effects on cell migration and tissue repair.
mechanism_brief: Thymosin beta-4 binds G-actin and may influence cell motility, angiogenesis, and inflammation in preclinical models; the fragment TB-500 is hypothesized to share some properties.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: Ipamorelin
what_it_is: A synthetic pentapeptide growth hormone secretagogue. Supplied as a lyophilized powder.
common_use_context: Studied in research settings as a selective growth hormone secretagogue.
mechanism_brief: Selective agonist of the ghrelin/growth hormone secretagogue receptor (GHS-R1a) in the pituitary, stimulating pulsatile growth hormone release with minimal effect on cortisol or prolactin.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: CJC-1295 (no DAC)
what_it_is: A synthetic analog of the first 29 amino acids of growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH), without the drug affinity complex (DAC). Supplied as a lyophilized powder.
common_use_context: Studied in research settings as a short-acting GHRH analog.
mechanism_brief: Binds the GHRH receptor on pituitary somatotrophs, stimulating short pulses of growth hormone release. Lacks the DAC modification that extends half-life.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: CJC-1295 with DAC
what_it_is: A long-acting GHRH analog incorporating a drug affinity complex (DAC) that binds serum albumin. Supplied as a lyophilized powder.
common_use_context: Studied in early clinical research as a long-acting GHRH analog.
mechanism_brief: Binds the GHRH receptor on pituitary somatotrophs. The DAC modification covalently binds albumin in circulation, dramatically extending half-life and producing sustained elevation of GH and IGF-1.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: Sermorelin
what_it_is: A synthetic peptide consisting of the first 29 amino acids of human growth hormone-releasing hormone. Supplied as a lyophilized powder.
common_use_context: Historically used in pediatric growth hormone deficiency diagnostic testing and treatment. Currently used off-label by some compounding pharmacies.
mechanism_brief: Binds GHRH receptors on pituitary somatotrophs, stimulating endogenous growth hormone release in a physiologic pulsatile pattern.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic or sterile water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Previously FDA-approved (Geref) for pediatric growth hormone deficiency; marketed product was discontinued. Currently available via compounding pharmacies.

name: Tesamorelin
what_it_is: A synthetic GHRH analog stabilized by an N-terminal trans-3-hexenoic acid modification. Supplied as a lyophilized powder.
common_use_context: Used in HIV-associated lipodystrophy to reduce excess abdominal fat.
mechanism_brief: Binds and activates pituitary GHRH receptors, stimulating endogenous growth hormone release and downstream IGF-1 production.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder; reconstituted with the supplied sterile water for injection prior to subcutaneous administration.
fda_approved_for: Reduction of excess abdominal fat in HIV-infected adult patients with lipodystrophy.

name: MOTS-c
aliases: Mitochondrial Open Reading Frame of the 12S rRNA-c
what_it_is: A 16-amino-acid peptide encoded within the mitochondrial 12S rRNA gene, considered a mitochondrially-derived peptide. Supplied as a lyophilized powder.
common_use_context: Studied in preclinical research for roles in metabolic regulation and mitochondrial function.
mechanism_brief: Translocates to the nucleus under metabolic stress and is hypothesized to regulate AMPK signaling and folate-methionine pathways, influencing glucose and lipid metabolism in preclinical models.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: 5-Amino-1MQ
what_it_is: A small-molecule research compound, not a true peptide despite community grouping. Available as a powder or capsule.
common_use_context: Studied in preclinical research for effects on adipose tissue metabolism.
mechanism_brief: Selective inhibitor of nicotinamide N-methyltransferase (NNMT), an enzyme implicated in adipocyte energy metabolism in preclinical models.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: GHK-Cu
what_it_is: A naturally occurring tripeptide (glycyl-L-histidyl-L-lysine) complexed with copper(II). Found in plasma and used in cosmetic formulations.
common_use_context: Used in topical cosmetic products marketed for skin appearance. Studied in research for wound healing properties.
mechanism_brief: GHK has high affinity for copper and serves as a carrier; the complex is proposed to modulate gene expression related to skin remodeling, collagen synthesis, and antioxidant defense in preclinical studies.
reconstitution_note: Lyophilized injectable forms require reconstitution with bacteriostatic water; topical cosmetic formulations are pre-mixed.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved as a drug; used as a cosmetic ingredient.

name: Selank
typical_storage: refrigerated; reconstituted vials kept at 2-8°C, usually used within 30-60 days
what_it_is: A synthetic heptapeptide analog of the human tetrapeptide tuftsin (an immunomodulatory fragment of IgG), developed by the Russian Academy of Medical Sciences.
common_use_context: Approved in Russia for generalized anxiety; outside Russia it is used off-label / as a research peptide. ROUTES: most commonly self-administered as an intranasal spray (typically 200-300 mcg per nostril, 1-3x/day) — research literature also describes subcutaneous injection at similar microgram doses, and sublingual oral dosing has been reported anecdotally with reduced bioavailability.
mechanism_brief: Proposed to modulate brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) expression and to enhance GABAergic and serotonergic signalling; preclinical work also reports an enkephalinase-inhibition / endogenous-opioid potentiation effect, hypothesized to underlie the anxiolytic profile.
reconstitution_note: Lyophilized vials reconstitute with bacteriostatic water (typically 2-3 mL per 5 mg vial → easy 100-200 mcg microdrops). Pre-mixed nasal sprays are also sold; for injection use, the same reconstitution applies.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound in the US.
source_citation: Russian State Pharmacopoeia; published preclinical literature

name: Semax
typical_storage: refrigerated; reconstituted vials kept at 2-8°C, used within 30-60 days
what_it_is: A synthetic heptapeptide (Met-Glu-His-Phe-Pro-Gly-Pro) derived from a fragment of adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH 4-10), developed by the Russian Academy of Medical Sciences in the 1980s.
common_use_context: Approved in Russia for ischemic stroke, transient ischemic attack, and cognitive disorders; not approved elsewhere. ROUTES: most commonly intranasal (typically 100-600 mcg per dose, 1-3x/day depending on formulation strength). Subcutaneous injection has been used in clinical studies. The N-acetyl variant (NA-Semax / Semax NA) is a longer-acting analog also dosed intranasally.
mechanism_brief: Modulates BDNF and nerve growth factor (NGF) expression, enhances monoaminergic neurotransmission (dopamine, serotonin) in cortical regions, and has been shown in preclinical work to potentiate the action of endogenous regulatory peptides — together giving it a nootropic + neuroprotective profile.
reconstitution_note: Lyophilized vials reconstitute with bacteriostatic water (typically 2-3 mL per 5 mg vial). For nasal use, transfer the reconstituted solution into a spray bottle or dose with a micropipette.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound in the US.
source_citation: Russian State Pharmacopoeia; published preclinical and clinical literature

name: Epitalon
what_it_is: A synthetic tetrapeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly) developed in Russia based on a sequence isolated from the pineal gland.
common_use_context: Studied in Russian research literature for effects on aging biomarkers; not approved as a drug outside that context.
mechanism_brief: Hypothesized in preclinical studies to influence telomerase activity and circadian regulation, though human pharmacokinetics are poorly characterized.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: DSIP
what_it_is: A nonapeptide originally isolated from rabbit cerebral venous blood during electrically induced sleep.
common_use_context: Studied in early research literature for effects on sleep architecture and stress response; no current clinical use.
mechanism_brief: Mechanism is poorly characterized; preclinical studies suggest modulation of GABAergic and neuroendocrine systems without binding to a specific identified receptor.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: AOD-9604
what_it_is: A synthetic peptide derived from the C-terminal region of human growth hormone (residues 176-191).
common_use_context: Investigated in early clinical trials for obesity but did not achieve regulatory approval as a drug.
mechanism_brief: Originally hypothesized to mimic the lipolytic activity of growth hormone without affecting growth or insulin sensitivity; subsequent clinical trials did not demonstrate efficacy.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound. Holds GRAS status as a food ingredient in some jurisdictions but not as a drug.

name: Hexarelin
what_it_is: A synthetic hexapeptide growth hormone secretagogue, structurally related to GHRP-6.
common_use_context: Studied in research as a potent growth hormone secretagogue.
mechanism_brief: Agonist of the ghrelin/growth hormone secretagogue receptor (GHS-R1a), stimulating pituitary GH release; also acts at cardiac CD36 receptors in preclinical studies.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: Melanotan II
what_it_is: A synthetic cyclic heptapeptide analog of alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone (alpha-MSH).
common_use_context: Studied in early research as a melanocortin receptor agonist; later development was discontinued in favor of derivatives such as bremelanotide and afamelanotide.
mechanism_brief: Non-selective agonist of melanocortin receptors (MC1R, MC3R, MC4R, MC5R). MC1R activation stimulates melanin synthesis; MC4R activation can influence sexual response and appetite.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: PT-141
what_it_is: A synthetic cyclic heptapeptide analog of alpha-MSH, developed as a metabolite of melanotan II. Supplied as a prefilled autoinjector (Vyleesi).
common_use_context: Used in premenopausal women for acquired, generalized hypoactive sexual desire disorder (HSDD).
mechanism_brief: Acts as a non-selective melanocortin receptor agonist with preferential activity at MC4R, which is implicated in central regulation of sexual response.
fda_approved_for: Treatment of premenopausal women with acquired, generalized hypoactive sexual desire disorder (HSDD).

name: Finasteride
what_it_is: A synthetic 4-azasteroid 5-alpha-reductase inhibitor available as oral tablets. Also available in compounded topical formulations.
common_use_context: Used in male pattern hair loss (androgenetic alopecia) and benign prostatic hyperplasia.
mechanism_brief: Selectively inhibits the type II isoform of 5-alpha-reductase, the enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), lowering scalp and serum DHT.
fda_approved_for: Male pattern hair loss (Propecia, 1 mg); benign prostatic hyperplasia (Proscar, 5 mg). Topical formulations are not FDA-approved.

name: Minoxidil
what_it_is: A vasodilator originally developed as an antihypertensive. Available as a topical solution or foam (Rogaine) and as oral tablets (Loniten).
common_use_context: Topical formulation is used for androgenetic alopecia in both sexes. Oral formulation is approved for severe hypertension and is used off-label at low doses for hair loss.
mechanism_brief: An ATP-sensitive potassium channel opener. The active metabolite minoxidil sulfate is believed to prolong the anagen phase of the hair growth cycle; the exact mechanism for hair growth remains incompletely understood.
fda_approved_for: Topical: androgenetic alopecia. Oral: severe hypertension. Low-dose oral use for hair loss is off-label.

name: Modafinil
what_it_is: A wakefulness-promoting agent available as oral tablets. Schedule IV controlled substance in the US.
common_use_context: Used to improve wakefulness in narcolepsy, obstructive sleep apnea with residual sleepiness, and shift work sleep disorder.
mechanism_brief: The exact mechanism is not fully characterized. Modafinil weakly inhibits the dopamine transporter and influences orexin, histaminergic, and noradrenergic systems involved in arousal.
fda_approved_for: Narcolepsy, obstructive sleep apnea (as adjunct to standard treatment), and shift work sleep disorder.

name: Adderall
what_it_is: A mixture of four amphetamine salts (dextroamphetamine and amphetamine) available as immediate-release tablets and extended-release capsules. Schedule II controlled substance.
common_use_context: Used in attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and narcolepsy.
mechanism_brief: Increases synaptic concentrations of dopamine and norepinephrine by promoting their release from presynaptic neurons and inhibiting their reuptake via the dopamine and norepinephrine transporters.
fda_approved_for: Attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and narcolepsy.

name: Bupropion
what_it_is: An aminoketone antidepressant chemically unrelated to other major antidepressant classes. Available as immediate-release, sustained-release, and extended-release tablets.
common_use_context: Used in major depressive disorder, seasonal affective disorder, and smoking cessation.
mechanism_brief: Norepinephrine-dopamine reuptake inhibitor (NDRI); inhibits the reuptake of dopamine and norepinephrine with minimal effect on serotonin reuptake.
fda_approved_for: Major depressive disorder, seasonal affective disorder (Wellbutrin XL), and smoking cessation (Zyban).

name: Phenibut
what_it_is: A synthetic GABA derivative with an added phenyl ring developed in the Soviet Union. Regulatory status varies by jurisdiction; not FDA-approved in the US and subject to import restrictions and state-level scheduling.
common_use_context: Used as a prescription medication in some former Soviet states for anxiety and related indications. Not approved as a medication in the US.
mechanism_brief: Acts as an agonist at GABA-B receptors and, at higher concentrations, GABA-A receptors. Also blocks alpha-2-delta calcium channels at higher doses.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved. Regulatory status varies; restricted or scheduled in several jurisdictions.

name: Levothyroxine
what_it_is: A synthetic form of the thyroid hormone thyroxine (T4) available as oral tablets, capsules, and an oral solution.
common_use_context: Used as replacement therapy in primary, secondary, and tertiary hypothyroidism and in the suppressive management of thyroid cancer.
mechanism_brief: Synthetic T4 is converted peripherally to the more active triiodothyronine (T3); both bind nuclear thyroid hormone receptors to regulate gene transcription affecting metabolism, growth, and development.
fda_approved_for: Hypothyroidism (all forms) and as an adjunct to surgery and radioiodine therapy in the management of thyrotropin-dependent well-differentiated thyroid cancer.

name: Metformin
what_it_is: A biguanide oral antihyperglycemic agent. Available as immediate-release and extended-release tablets and an oral solution.
common_use_context: Used in type 2 diabetes mellitus. Also studied off-label in polycystic ovary syndrome and as an investigational compound in aging research.
mechanism_brief: Decreases hepatic glucose production primarily by inhibiting mitochondrial complex I and activating AMPK, and improves peripheral insulin sensitivity. It does not stimulate insulin secretion.
fda_approved_for: Type 2 diabetes mellitus in adults and pediatric patients aged 10 and older.

name: Methylene Blue
what_it_is: A phenothiazine dye with a long history of medical use. Available as a sterile injectable solution (ProvayBlue) and as a chemical reagent in various oral preparations.
common_use_context: Used clinically as an injection for acquired methemoglobinemia. Studied in research for various applications including infection and neurodegeneration.
mechanism_brief: At low concentrations, reduces methemoglobin to hemoglobin by accepting electrons from NADPH via NADPH-methemoglobin reductase. Also inhibits monoamine oxidase and modulates mitochondrial electron transport.
fda_approved_for: Treatment of pediatric and adult patients with acquired methemoglobinemia (intravenous injectable form).

name: Low-Dose Naltrexone
what_it_is: Naltrexone is an opioid receptor antagonist. The low-dose variant typically refers to compounded preparations at a fraction of the standard dose.
common_use_context: Standard-dose naltrexone is FDA-approved for opioid and alcohol use disorders. Low-dose use is off-label, studied in research for autoimmune and chronic pain conditions.
mechanism_brief: At standard doses, competitively blocks mu-opioid receptors. The proposed low-dose mechanism involves transient opioid receptor blockade leading to compensatory upregulation of endogenous opioids and modulation of glial Toll-like receptor 4.
fda_approved_for: Standard-dose naltrexone is FDA-approved for opioid use disorder and alcohol use disorder. Low-dose use is off-label, typically via compounding pharmacies.

name: Ostarine
what_it_is: An investigational non-steroidal selective androgen receptor modulator (SARM) studied in oral form.
common_use_context: Investigated in clinical trials for muscle wasting and related conditions; did not achieve regulatory approval. Listed on the WADA prohibited list.
mechanism_brief: Tissue-selective partial agonist of the androgen receptor, with preferential anabolic activity in muscle and bone and reduced activity in androgenic tissues such as prostate.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; investigational compound. Sale as a dietary supplement is prohibited by the FDA.

name: RAD-140
what_it_is: An investigational non-steroidal selective androgen receptor modulator (SARM) studied in oral form.
common_use_context: Investigated in early-phase clinical research for muscle wasting and breast cancer. Listed on the WADA prohibited list.
mechanism_brief: Selective androgen receptor agonist with preferential activity at androgen receptors in muscle and bone relative to prostate and other androgenic tissues.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; investigational compound. Sale as a dietary supplement is prohibited by the FDA.

name: Vitamin D3
what_it_is: The naturally occurring form of vitamin D produced in skin upon UVB exposure. Available as oral capsules, tablets, and liquid drops.
common_use_context: Used for vitamin D deficiency, bone health, and as a general dietary supplement.
mechanism_brief: Converted in the liver to 25-hydroxyvitamin D and in the kidney to 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D (calcitriol), the active hormone, which binds the vitamin D receptor to regulate calcium homeostasis and gene expression.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement at typical strengths. Prescription forms are used for documented deficiency states.

name: Vitamin K2
what_it_is: A fat-soluble vitamin in the menaquinone family. MK-4 and MK-7 are the most common subtypes in supplements.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement, often paired with vitamin D, for bone and cardiovascular support.
mechanism_brief: Serves as a cofactor for gamma-glutamyl carboxylase, which activates vitamin K-dependent proteins such as osteocalcin and matrix Gla-protein, involved in bone mineralization and vascular calcium handling.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: Magnesium Glycinate
what_it_is: A chelated form of magnesium bound to two glycine molecules, available as oral capsules and tablets.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement for general magnesium intake.
mechanism_brief: Provides elemental magnesium, a cofactor in over 300 enzymatic reactions involving ATP, nucleic acid synthesis, and neuromuscular function.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: Zinc
what_it_is: An essential trace mineral available in various salt forms as oral supplements.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement for zinc intake, immune support, and to address documented deficiency.
mechanism_brief: Acts as a structural and catalytic cofactor for hundreds of enzymes and transcription factors, including those involved in immune function, DNA synthesis, and hormone metabolism.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: Creatine Monohydrate
what_it_is: A naturally occurring nitrogenous organic acid available as a powder, capsule, or tablet supplement.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement in athletic and resistance-training contexts.
mechanism_brief: Increases intramuscular phosphocreatine stores, supporting rapid regeneration of ATP from ADP during short-duration high-intensity contraction via the creatine kinase reaction.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: Mazdutide
what_it_is: An investigational dual glucagon and GLP-1 receptor agonist under clinical development as a once-weekly subcutaneous injection.
common_use_context: Under investigation for obesity and type 2 diabetes in clinical trials, with later-stage development concentrated in China.
mechanism_brief: Co-agonism of glucagon and GLP-1 receptors, combining incretin-mediated effects on insulin secretion and satiety with glucagon-mediated effects on hepatic glucose handling and energy expenditure.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; investigational compound in clinical trials.

name: Survodutide
what_it_is: An investigational dual glucagon and GLP-1 receptor agonist under clinical development as a once-weekly subcutaneous injection.
common_use_context: Under investigation for obesity, type 2 diabetes, and metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH) in clinical trials.
mechanism_brief: Balanced co-agonism of glucagon and GLP-1 receptors, intended to combine appetite suppression with increased energy expenditure and hepatic fat reduction.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; investigational compound in clinical trials.

name: Pemvidutide
what_it_is: An investigational dual glucagon and GLP-1 receptor agonist under clinical development as a once-weekly subcutaneous injection.
common_use_context: Under investigation primarily for metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH) and obesity in clinical trials.
mechanism_brief: Co-agonism of glucagon and GLP-1 receptors with a balance designed to drive hepatic fat reduction and weight loss while supporting glycemic effects.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; investigational compound in clinical trials.

name: Setmelanotide
what_it_is: A synthetic cyclic peptide melanocortin-4 receptor agonist available as a once-daily subcutaneous injection.
common_use_context: Used in chronic weight management in patients with monogenic obesity due to confirmed POMC, PCSK1, LEPR, or Bardet-Biedl syndrome deficiencies.
mechanism_brief: Selective agonist of the melanocortin-4 receptor (MC4R), restoring downstream signaling in the central leptin-melanocortin pathway involved in energy balance.
fda_approved_for: Chronic weight management in adult and pediatric patients aged 2 and older with obesity due to POMC, PCSK1, or LEPR deficiency confirmed by genetic testing, or Bardet-Biedl syndrome.

name: Bimagrumab
what_it_is: An investigational fully human monoclonal antibody against activin type II receptors, administered by intravenous or subcutaneous infusion in clinical trials.
common_use_context: Under investigation for obesity (in combination with incretin therapies) and sarcopenia-related muscle wasting in clinical trials.
mechanism_brief: Blocks activin type IIA and IIB receptors, antagonizing myostatin and related ligand signaling and producing increased skeletal muscle mass alongside reductions in fat mass in trial subjects.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; investigational compound in clinical trials.

name: Gonadorelin
what_it_is: A synthetic decapeptide identical to endogenous gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH). Supplied as a lyophilized powder or sterile solution.
common_use_context: Historically used in diagnostic testing of pituitary function and in pulsatile therapy for hypothalamic hypogonadism. Compounded gonadorelin has become a common HCG alternative in US testosterone-therapy clinics.
mechanism_brief: Binds GnRH receptors on pituitary gonadotrophs, stimulating release of luteinizing hormone (LH) and follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH). LH in turn stimulates testicular Leydig cell testosterone production.
reconstitution_note: Compounded lyophilized formulations are reconstituted with bacteriostatic water prior to subcutaneous injection.
fda_approved_for: Previously FDA-approved (Factrel) for diagnostic evaluation of pituitary gonadotropic function; original brand discontinued. Currently available primarily through compounding pharmacies in the US.

name: Kisspeptin
what_it_is: A neuropeptide encoded by the KISS1 gene. The truncated kisspeptin-10 fragment is the form most commonly used in clinical research.
common_use_context: Studied in clinical research as an upstream stimulator of the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis and as an investigational diagnostic agent in hypogonadotropic hypogonadism.
mechanism_brief: Activates the KISS1R (GPR54) receptor on hypothalamic GnRH neurons, triggering pulsatile GnRH release and downstream LH and FSH secretion.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; investigational compound.

name: Pregnenolone
what_it_is: An endogenous steroid synthesized from cholesterol and acting as the metabolic precursor to most other steroid hormones. Available as an over-the-counter oral supplement in the US.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement and studied in research for cognitive, mood, and neurosteroid-related effects.
mechanism_brief: Serves as an upstream substrate for steroidogenesis (DHEA, progesterone, mineralocorticoids, glucocorticoids, and sex steroids) and also acts directly as a neurosteroid modulating GABA-A and NMDA receptor function.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement in the US; not FDA-approved as a drug.

name: DHEA
what_it_is: An endogenous adrenal steroid serving as a precursor to androgens and estrogens. Available over-the-counter as an oral supplement and by prescription as an intravaginal insert (Intrarosa).
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement and studied in research for adrenal insufficiency, mood, and age-related hormonal decline. Intrarosa is FDA-approved for postmenopausal dyspareunia.
mechanism_brief: Converted peripherally to androstenedione and onward to testosterone and estradiol via steroidogenic enzymes. Also has direct neurosteroid effects at GABA-A and sigma receptors.
fda_approved_for: Prasterone (Intrarosa, intravaginal insert) is FDA-approved for moderate-to-severe dyspareunia due to vulvar and vaginal atrophy of menopause. Oral DHEA is regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: Letrozole
what_it_is: A non-steroidal triazole aromatase inhibitor available as an oral tablet.
common_use_context: Used in postmenopausal women with hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer. Off-label, sometimes used by clinicians for ovulation induction or to manage elevated estradiol in men on testosterone therapy.
mechanism_brief: Reversibly binds the heme group of the aromatase (CYP19) enzyme, blocking conversion of androgens to estrogens and substantially lowering circulating estradiol.
fda_approved_for: Adjuvant and first/second-line treatment of hormone receptor-positive breast cancer in postmenopausal women.

name: Exemestane
what_it_is: A steroidal irreversible aromatase inactivator available as an oral tablet.
common_use_context: Used in postmenopausal women with estrogen-receptor-positive breast cancer. Off-label, sometimes used by clinicians to manage elevated estradiol in men on testosterone therapy.
mechanism_brief: An androstenedione analog that binds covalently to the active site of aromatase (CYP19), permanently inactivating the enzyme and reducing peripheral conversion of androgens to estrogens.
fda_approved_for: Adjuvant treatment of estrogen-receptor-positive early breast cancer and treatment of advanced breast cancer in postmenopausal women.

name: Somatropin
what_it_is: A recombinant DNA-derived 191-amino-acid polypeptide identical in sequence to endogenous human growth hormone. Multiple brand-name products are available.
common_use_context: Used in pediatric and adult growth hormone deficiency, several pediatric growth disorders (Turner syndrome, SHOX deficiency, Noonan syndrome, idiopathic short stature, Prader-Willi syndrome, small for gestational age), and AIDS-related wasting and HIV lipodystrophy.
mechanism_brief: Binds the growth hormone receptor on hepatic and peripheral tissues, activating the JAK2/STAT5 pathway and stimulating IGF-1 production, protein synthesis, lipolysis, and longitudinal bone growth in pediatric patients.
reconstitution_note: Many formulations are supplied as lyophilized powder reconstituted with the supplied diluent; some products are pre-mixed liquid cartridges. Always reconstituted with the manufacturer-supplied diluent.
fda_approved_for: Pediatric and adult growth hormone deficiency, Turner syndrome, Prader-Willi syndrome, Noonan syndrome, SHOX deficiency, idiopathic short stature, small for gestational age, AIDS-related wasting, and short bowel syndrome (varies by brand).

name: Somapacitan
what_it_is: A long-acting growth hormone analog with a non-covalent albumin-binding side chain, administered as a once-weekly subcutaneous injection via prefilled pen.
common_use_context: Used in adult and pediatric growth hormone deficiency as a less-frequent alternative to daily somatropin.
mechanism_brief: Binds the growth hormone receptor and activates the same downstream JAK2/STAT5 pathway as endogenous GH. The albumin-binding side chain extends half-life and supports once-weekly dosing.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a pre-mixed solution in a prefilled pen; no reconstitution required.
fda_approved_for: Replacement of endogenous growth hormone in adults and pediatric patients aged 2.5 years and older with growth hormone deficiency.

name: GHRP-2
what_it_is: A synthetic hexapeptide growth hormone secretagogue. Supplied as a lyophilized powder.
common_use_context: Approved in Japan as a diagnostic agent for growth hormone deficiency. Studied in research as a GH secretagogue elsewhere.
mechanism_brief: Agonist of the ghrelin/growth hormone secretagogue receptor (GHS-R1a) on pituitary somatotrophs, stimulating pulsatile growth hormone release. Can also modestly increase prolactin and cortisol.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound in the US. Approved as a diagnostic agent in Japan.

name: GHRP-6
what_it_is: A synthetic hexapeptide growth hormone secretagogue, the prototypical ghrelin receptor agonist of its class. Supplied as a lyophilized powder.
common_use_context: Studied in research as a GH secretagogue. Noted in early literature for marked appetite stimulation via ghrelin-receptor activity.
mechanism_brief: Agonist of the ghrelin/growth hormone secretagogue receptor (GHS-R1a) on pituitary somatotrophs and hypothalamic neurons, stimulating GH release and appetite.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: MK-677
what_it_is: An orally bioavailable non-peptide growth hormone secretagogue, studied historically as an investigational drug.
common_use_context: Investigated in clinical trials for sarcopenia, hip-fracture recovery, and frailty; did not achieve regulatory approval. Listed on the WADA prohibited list.
mechanism_brief: Selective agonist of the ghrelin/growth hormone secretagogue receptor (GHS-R1a), mimicking ghrelin to stimulate GH and IGF-1 release and increase appetite.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; investigational compound. Sale as a dietary supplement is prohibited by the FDA.

name: IGF-1 LR3
what_it_is: A synthetic 83-amino-acid analog of insulin-like growth factor 1 with an N-terminal 13-residue extension and an arginine-3 substitution. Supplied as a lyophilized powder.
common_use_context: Used historically as a research reagent and in cell culture. Studied in preclinical research for IGF-1 receptor signaling.
mechanism_brief: Agonist of the IGF-1 receptor. The arginine-3 substitution and N-terminal extension dramatically reduce binding to IGF binding proteins, prolonging free circulation and bioactivity relative to native IGF-1.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water or an acidic diluent prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: IGF-1 DES
what_it_is: A truncated analog of IGF-1 lacking the first three N-terminal amino acids. Supplied as a lyophilized powder.
common_use_context: Used historically as a research reagent. Studied in preclinical research for IGF-1 receptor signaling with reduced binding-protein interaction.
mechanism_brief: Agonist of the IGF-1 receptor with reduced affinity for IGF binding proteins, leading to a short, intense burst of receptor activation when administered.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water or an acidic diluent prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: Thymosin Alpha-1
what_it_is: A synthetic 28-amino-acid peptide identical in sequence to a naturally occurring thymic peptide. Supplied as a lyophilized powder (Zadaxin).
common_use_context: Approved in multiple countries outside the US for chronic hepatitis B and C and as an immune adjuvant. In the US, available only through compounding pharmacies.
mechanism_brief: Modulates T-cell maturation and function and influences innate immune signaling, including Toll-like receptor pathways, in preclinical and clinical studies.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder reconstituted with the supplied or bacteriostatic water prior to subcutaneous injection.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; available through compounding pharmacies in the US. Approved in numerous other countries for chronic hepatitis B and C.

name: LL-37
what_it_is: A synthetic 37-amino-acid cationic antimicrobial peptide identical to the active C-terminal fragment of human cathelicidin (hCAP-18). Supplied as a lyophilized powder.
common_use_context: Studied in preclinical and early clinical research as an antimicrobial and immunomodulatory peptide.
mechanism_brief: Disrupts microbial membranes via electrostatic interaction with anionic phospholipids. Also modulates innate immune signaling, chemotaxis, and angiogenesis in preclinical models.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: KPV
what_it_is: A synthetic tripeptide corresponding to the C-terminal three residues (lysine-proline-valine) of alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone.
common_use_context: Studied in preclinical research for anti-inflammatory effects, particularly in the gastrointestinal tract.
mechanism_brief: Proposed in preclinical studies to enter cells via PepT1 transporters and suppress NF-kappaB signaling and pro-inflammatory cytokine release in epithelial and immune cells.
reconstitution_note: Lyophilized injectable forms require reconstitution with bacteriostatic water; oral capsule forms are pre-mixed.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: SS-31
what_it_is: An investigational synthetic tetrapeptide that concentrates in the inner mitochondrial membrane. Supplied as a lyophilized powder or sterile solution in clinical trials.
common_use_context: Under investigation for primary mitochondrial diseases including Barth syndrome and for dry age-related macular degeneration.
mechanism_brief: Selectively binds cardiolipin in the inner mitochondrial membrane, stabilizing cristae architecture and improving electron transport chain efficiency and ATP production in preclinical models.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with the supplied diluent prior to subcutaneous injection in trials.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; investigational compound in clinical trials.

name: AICAR
what_it_is: A small-molecule AMP analog and AMP-activated protein kinase activator, not a true peptide despite community grouping. Supplied as a lyophilized powder.
common_use_context: Studied in preclinical and early clinical research for metabolic and cardioprotective effects. Listed on the WADA prohibited list.
mechanism_brief: Phosphorylated intracellularly to ZMP, which mimics AMP and allosterically activates AMP-activated protein kinase (AMPK), influencing fatty acid oxidation, glucose uptake, and mitochondrial biogenesis in preclinical models.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with sterile water or bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: Cerebrolysin
what_it_is: A peptide preparation derived from enzymatic hydrolysis of porcine brain tissue, containing a defined mixture of low-molecular-weight peptides and free amino acids. Supplied as a sterile solution.
common_use_context: Approved in several countries including Russia, China, Mexico, and parts of Europe for stroke, dementia, and traumatic brain injury. Not approved in the US.
mechanism_brief: Proposed in preclinical and clinical studies to exert neurotrophic effects mimicking endogenous neurotrophic factors, supporting neuronal survival and synaptic plasticity.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a pre-mixed sterile solution; no reconstitution required.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; not marketed in the US.

name: Cortexin
what_it_is: A peptide preparation derived from bovine or porcine cerebral cortex, developed in Russia. Supplied as a lyophilized powder for intramuscular injection.
common_use_context: Approved in Russia and several neighboring countries for stroke, encephalopathy, and cognitive impairment. Not approved in the US.
mechanism_brief: Proposed in Russian research literature to exert neurotrophic and neuroprotective effects via a mixture of low-molecular-weight cortex-derived peptides, though mechanisms are not well characterized.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder reconstituted with sterile water or saline prior to intramuscular injection.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound in the US.

name: Pinealon
what_it_is: A synthetic tripeptide (Glu-Asp-Arg) developed in Russia as one of the Khavinson short peptides, derived from pineal-gland-related sequences.
common_use_context: Studied in Russian research literature for proposed effects on cognition and aging biomarkers; not approved as a drug outside that context.
mechanism_brief: Hypothesized in preclinical studies to penetrate cell nuclei and modulate gene expression and oxidative stress, though human pharmacokinetics are poorly characterized.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: Vesugen
what_it_is: A synthetic tripeptide (Lys-Glu-Asp) developed in Russia as a Khavinson short peptide intended to target vascular tissue.
common_use_context: Studied in Russian research literature for proposed vascular and endothelial effects; not approved as a drug outside that context.
mechanism_brief: Hypothesized in preclinical studies to modulate gene expression in endothelial and vascular smooth muscle cells, though human pharmacokinetics are poorly characterized.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: Cardiogen
what_it_is: A synthetic tetrapeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp-Arg) developed in Russia as a Khavinson short peptide intended to target cardiac tissue.
common_use_context: Studied in Russian research literature for proposed effects on cardiac aging biomarkers; not approved as a drug outside that context.
mechanism_brief: Hypothesized in preclinical studies to influence gene expression in cardiomyocytes, though human pharmacokinetics are poorly characterized.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: Prostamax
what_it_is: A peptide preparation derived from animal prostate tissue, developed in Russia as one of several organ-derived peptide bioregulators.
common_use_context: Used in Russia as a supplement-style preparation marketed for prostate-related concerns; not approved as a drug outside that context.
mechanism_brief: Hypothesized in Russian research literature to provide tissue-specific peptide signals that modulate prostate cell function, though mechanisms are not well characterized.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound in the US.

name: Lion's Mane
what_it_is: An edible medicinal mushroom (Hericium erinaceus) sold as a dietary supplement in capsule, powder, or tincture form.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement marketed for cognitive support. Studied in preclinical and small clinical trials for nerve growth factor-related effects.
mechanism_brief: Contains hericenones and erinacines, compounds shown in preclinical studies to stimulate nerve growth factor (NGF) expression and influence neuronal survival.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: L-Theanine
what_it_is: A non-proteinogenic amino acid found primarily in tea leaves (Camellia sinensis). Available as oral capsules and tablets.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement marketed for relaxation and as a complement to caffeine for focused calm.
mechanism_brief: Crosses the blood-brain barrier and is proposed to modulate glutamate and GABA neurotransmission and increase alpha-wave activity on EEG in clinical studies.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement; recognized as GRAS for food use.

name: Rhodiola Rosea
what_it_is: A flowering plant in the Crassulaceae family. The root extract is sold as a dietary supplement, typically standardized to rosavins and salidroside.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement classified as an adaptogen. Studied in small clinical trials for fatigue, stress, and mood.
mechanism_brief: Active constituents (salidroside, rosavins) are proposed in preclinical studies to modulate monoamine neurotransmission, the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, and AMPK and mTOR signaling.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: Ashwagandha
what_it_is: A shrub in the Solanaceae family used in Ayurvedic tradition. The root extract is sold as a dietary supplement, often standardized to withanolides.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement classified as an adaptogen. Studied in clinical trials for stress, sleep, and small effects on serum testosterone in men.
mechanism_brief: Withanolides are proposed in preclinical studies to modulate the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, GABAergic signaling, and inflammatory mediators.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: Tongkat Ali
what_it_is: A flowering plant native to Southeast Asia. The root extract is sold as a dietary supplement, often standardized to eurycomanone or as a water-soluble extract (e.g., Physta).
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement marketed for male hormonal support. Studied in small clinical trials for effects on serum testosterone and SHBG.
mechanism_brief: Quassinoids (including eurycomanone) are proposed in preclinical and clinical studies to modulate steroidogenic enzymes and reduce SHBG, with downstream effects on free testosterone.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: Fadogia Agrestis
what_it_is: A West African shrub in the Rubiaceae family. The stem extract is sold as a dietary supplement, often as a 10:1 extract.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement marketed for male hormonal support. Human clinical data are limited; rodent toxicity findings (including testicular damage at higher doses) have been reported in preclinical literature.
mechanism_brief: Preclinical rat studies report increases in serum testosterone, hypothesized to involve LH-mimetic activity, though mechanisms in humans are not characterized.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement; human safety data are limited.

name: 9-Me-BC
what_it_is: A small-molecule beta-carboline derivative studied in preclinical neuropharmacology research. Available as a powder.
common_use_context: Studied in preclinical rodent research for proposed effects on dopaminergic signaling and neuroprotection.
mechanism_brief: Preclinical studies suggest weak monoamine oxidase inhibition along with effects on mitochondrial complex I, dopamine neuron survival, and neurogenesis-related gene expression in rodents.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: NSI-189
what_it_is: A synthetic small molecule of the benzylpiperazine-aminopyridine class, studied historically as an investigational antidepressant.
common_use_context: Investigated in Phase 1 and 2 clinical trials for major depressive disorder; did not achieve regulatory approval. No longer in active development.
mechanism_brief: Proposed in preclinical studies to stimulate hippocampal neurogenesis and increase hippocampal volume in rodent models, though the exact molecular target is not fully characterized.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; investigational compound, development discontinued.

name: Bromantane
what_it_is: A synthetic adamantane derivative developed in Russia. Available as oral tablets where marketed.
common_use_context: Approved in Russia for asthenic disorders. Used historically by some athletes; listed on the WADA prohibited list as a stimulant.
mechanism_brief: Proposed in preclinical studies to enhance dopamine synthesis via upregulation of tyrosine hydroxylase and to modulate GABA-ergic signaling, producing combined stimulant and anxiolytic effects.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; not marketed in the US.

name: Phenylpiracetam
what_it_is: A phenyl-substituted analog of piracetam developed in Russia. Available as oral tablets where marketed.
common_use_context: Approved in Russia for several neurological indications including stroke recovery. Listed on the WADA prohibited list as a stimulant.
mechanism_brief: Proposed in preclinical studies to modulate cholinergic, glutamatergic, and dopaminergic neurotransmission, with mild stimulant properties. Exact molecular targets remain incompletely characterized.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; not marketed in the US.

name: Magnesium L-Threonate
what_it_is: A magnesium salt of L-threonic acid, marketed as a brain-penetrant form of magnesium. Available as oral capsules and powders.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement marketed for cognitive and sleep support. Studied in small clinical trials for cognitive endpoints.
mechanism_brief: Preclinical rodent studies indicate that the threonate form raises cerebrospinal magnesium more effectively than other salts, with downstream effects on NMDA receptor signaling and synaptic plasticity.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: Apigenin
what_it_is: A naturally occurring flavone found in parsley, chamomile, and other plants. Available as oral capsules.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement marketed for sleep and relaxation. Studied in preclinical research for various flavonoid effects.
mechanism_brief: Acts as a partial agonist at benzodiazepine sites on the GABA-A receptor in preclinical studies, with additional effects on CD38, sirtuins, and various kinases described in vitro.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: Glycine
what_it_is: The simplest amino acid, available as an oral powder or tablet supplement.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement marketed for sleep quality and as a component of collagen synthesis.
mechanism_brief: Acts as an inhibitory neurotransmitter via glycine receptors in the spinal cord and brainstem and as a co-agonist at NMDA receptors. Also serves as a substrate for collagen and glutathione synthesis.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement; recognized as GRAS for food use.

name: Phosphatidylserine
what_it_is: A phospholipid component of cell membranes. Available as oral softgels and capsules, typically sourced from soy or sunflower lecithin.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement marketed for cognitive function and cortisol modulation in athletic contexts.
mechanism_brief: Incorporated into neuronal membranes, where it influences membrane fluidity and signal transduction. Clinical studies report modest effects on stress cortisol response.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: 5-HTP
what_it_is: An aromatic amino acid intermediate in the biosynthesis of serotonin. Available as an oral capsule supplement in the US.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement marketed for mood and sleep. Available as a prescription drug (Oxitriptan) in some other countries.
mechanism_brief: Crosses the blood-brain barrier and is decarboxylated to serotonin (5-HT), increasing central serotonergic tone. Peripheral conversion can produce gastrointestinal effects.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement in the US; prescription drug in some other countries.

name: L-Tryptophan
what_it_is: An essential aromatic amino acid required for protein synthesis and serving as the precursor to serotonin, melatonin, and niacin. Available as oral capsules and tablets.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement marketed for sleep and mood.
mechanism_brief: Crosses the blood-brain barrier via the large neutral amino acid transporter and is hydroxylated to 5-HTP, then decarboxylated to serotonin. Also feeds into the kynurenine pathway peripherally.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: Telmisartan
what_it_is: An angiotensin II receptor blocker (ARB) available as oral tablets.
common_use_context: Used in hypertension and cardiovascular risk reduction. Discussed in research literature for partial PPAR-gamma agonism and metabolic effects.
mechanism_brief: Selectively blocks the AT1 receptor for angiotensin II, lowering blood pressure. Also functions as a partial agonist of PPAR-gamma, influencing insulin sensitivity and lipid metabolism.
fda_approved_for: Hypertension and reduction of cardiovascular risk in patients at high cardiovascular risk who are unable to take ACE inhibitors.

name: Tadalafil
what_it_is: A long-acting phosphodiesterase type 5 (PDE5) inhibitor available as oral tablets.
common_use_context: Used in erectile dysfunction, benign prostatic hyperplasia, and pulmonary arterial hypertension. Low-dose daily formulations are FDA-approved for ED and BPH.
mechanism_brief: Selectively inhibits PDE5, increasing cyclic GMP in smooth muscle and producing vasodilation in the corpus cavernosum, prostate, bladder neck, and pulmonary vasculature.
fda_approved_for: Erectile dysfunction (Cialis, including as-needed and once-daily dosing), benign prostatic hyperplasia (Cialis once-daily), and pulmonary arterial hypertension (Adcirca, Alyq).

name: Citrulline Malate
what_it_is: A combination of the non-proteinogenic amino acid L-citrulline with malic acid, commonly in a 2:1 ratio. Available as oral powders and capsules.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement in athletic and resistance-training contexts.
mechanism_brief: L-citrulline is converted to L-arginine in the kidneys, raising plasma arginine and supporting nitric oxide synthesis and vasodilation. Malate serves as a tricarboxylic acid cycle intermediate.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: Beta-Alanine
what_it_is: A non-proteinogenic beta amino acid. Available as oral powders, capsules, and tablets.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement in athletic and resistance-training contexts; characteristic paresthesia at higher single doses is common.
mechanism_brief: Rate-limiting precursor for the synthesis of intramuscular carnosine (beta-alanyl-L-histidine), which buffers intracellular hydrogen ions during high-intensity contraction.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: Cordyceps
what_it_is: A genus of parasitic fungi cultivated for medicinal use. Sold as a dietary supplement in capsule, powder, or extract form.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement marketed for energy and exercise performance. Studied in small clinical trials for aerobic exercise endpoints.
mechanism_brief: Active constituents (cordycepin, polysaccharides) are proposed in preclinical studies to influence ATP production, AMPK signaling, and immune function.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: CoQ10
what_it_is: A lipid-soluble benzoquinone present in mitochondrial membranes. Available as oral softgels and capsules in oxidized (ubiquinone) and reduced (ubiquinol) forms.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement marketed for cardiovascular and mitochondrial support, particularly in statin users.
mechanism_brief: Functions as an electron carrier between mitochondrial complexes I/II and III in the electron transport chain and acts as a lipid-soluble antioxidant in cell membranes.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: Topical Minoxidil
what_it_is: A topical solution or foam formulation of minoxidil at 2% or 5% concentration applied directly to the scalp.
common_use_context: Used in androgenetic alopecia in both sexes. Most widely used over-the-counter treatment for pattern hair loss.
mechanism_brief: After topical application, scalp sulfotransferases convert minoxidil to its active metabolite minoxidil sulfate, an ATP-sensitive potassium channel opener proposed to prolong the anagen phase of hair growth.
fda_approved_for: Androgenetic alopecia (topical 2% and 5% formulations available over-the-counter).

name: Topical Finasteride
what_it_is: A compounded topical formulation of finasteride in a vehicle such as alcohol, propylene glycol, or liposomal base, applied directly to the scalp.
common_use_context: Used off-label for androgenetic alopecia via compounding pharmacies and telehealth services, intended to reduce systemic exposure relative to oral dosing.
mechanism_brief: Inhibits type II 5-alpha-reductase in scalp tissue, reducing local DHT. Some systemic absorption occurs with measurable serum levels, though typically lower than with oral administration.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved as a topical formulation; available through compounding pharmacies.

name: Tretinoin
what_it_is: A naturally occurring carboxylic acid form of vitamin A available as a topical cream, gel, or microsphere preparation, and as an oral capsule for oncologic use.
common_use_context: Topical formulations are used in acne vulgaris and photoaging. Oral tretinoin (Vesanoid) is used in acute promyelocytic leukemia.
mechanism_brief: Binds nuclear retinoic acid receptors (RAR alpha, beta, gamma) to modulate gene transcription, influencing keratinocyte differentiation, comedone formation, collagen synthesis, and melanogenesis.
fda_approved_for: Topical: acne vulgaris and palliation of fine wrinkles, mottled hyperpigmentation, and roughness of facial skin. Oral (Vesanoid): induction of remission in acute promyelocytic leukemia.

name: RU58841
what_it_is: A non-steroidal anti-androgen studied historically for topical use in androgenetic alopecia and acne. Development was discontinued; available only as a research chemical.
common_use_context: Investigated in early-stage research for androgenetic alopecia; never advanced to approval. Used outside any clinical framework via research-chemical suppliers.
mechanism_brief: Competitive antagonist of the androgen receptor at the level of the hair follicle, blocking DHT and testosterone binding to inhibit androgen-driven follicle miniaturization in preclinical models.
reconstitution_note: Typically dissolved by users in alcohol or propylene-glycol carriers for topical application; no standardized pharmaceutical formulation exists.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compound.

name: Urolithin A
what_it_is: A gut microbial metabolite of ellagitannins (found in pomegranate, walnuts, and certain berries). Available as a dietary supplement (Mitopure) holding FDA GRAS status as a food ingredient.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement marketed for mitochondrial and muscle health. Studied in clinical trials for skeletal muscle endpoints in older adults.
mechanism_brief: Induces mitophagy (selective autophagy of damaged mitochondria) in preclinical models, leading to improved mitochondrial quality and skeletal muscle function in animal and human studies.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement; holds GRAS status as a food ingredient.

name: Spermidine
what_it_is: A naturally occurring polyamine found in wheat germ and other foods. Available as a dietary supplement in capsule or wheat-germ-extract form.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement marketed for longevity and autophagy support. Studied in observational and small clinical trials for cardiovascular and cognitive endpoints.
mechanism_brief: Induces autophagy in preclinical models through inhibition of acetyltransferase EP300 and other mechanisms, with downstream effects on cellular proteostasis and lipid metabolism.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: NMN
what_it_is: A direct biosynthetic precursor to nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (NAD+). Regulatory status has shifted; in 2022 the FDA stated NMN is no longer permitted as a dietary supplement after being a drug investigational article, though enforcement is variable.
common_use_context: Sold as a dietary supplement and studied in clinical trials for effects on NAD+ levels and metabolic endpoints in aging adults.
mechanism_brief: Converted intracellularly to NAD+, a redox cofactor required by sirtuins, PARPs, and CD38 and by mitochondrial electron transport. NMN supplementation raises circulating and intracellular NAD+ in clinical studies.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; regulatory status as a dietary supplement is contested following FDA review.

name: Nicotinamide Riboside
what_it_is: A form of vitamin B3 and a precursor to NAD+. Available as a dietary supplement (Niagen) holding FDA New Dietary Ingredient and GRAS status.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement marketed for NAD+ support and cellular energy. Studied in clinical trials for cardiometabolic and neurologic endpoints.
mechanism_brief: Converted intracellularly via NRK enzymes to NMN and then to NAD+, raising NAD+ levels in tissues. NAD+ supports sirtuin, PARP, and mitochondrial function.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement; holds GRAS status as a food ingredient.

name: Berberine
what_it_is: An isoquinoline alkaloid found in plants such as Berberis (barberry) and goldenseal. Available as oral capsule and tablet supplements.
common_use_context: Used as a dietary supplement marketed for glucose and lipid support. Studied in clinical trials with modest effects on hemoglobin A1c, LDL cholesterol, and blood pressure.
mechanism_brief: Activates AMP-activated protein kinase (AMPK) and inhibits mitochondrial complex I in preclinical models, producing effects on glucose uptake, lipogenesis, and gut microbiome composition.
fda_approved_for: Regulated as a dietary supplement.

name: GHK-Cu (Injectable)
what_it_is: The copper tripeptide GHK-Cu supplied as a lyophilized powder for subcutaneous injection, as opposed to the pre-mixed topical cosmetic form.
common_use_context: Used by some via subcutaneous injection in research/community settings; the same tripeptide is also sold in topical skincare formulations.
mechanism_brief: GHK has high affinity for copper and serves as a carrier; the complex is proposed to modulate genes related to skin remodeling, collagen synthesis, and antioxidant defense in preclinical studies.
reconstitution_note: Lyophilized injectable forms require reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved as a drug; used as a cosmetic ingredient in topical form.

name: CJC-1295 / Ipamorelin
what_it_is: A community blend combining a GHRH analog (commonly CJC-1295 without DAC / Mod GRF 1-29) with the ghrelin-receptor agonist Ipamorelin, supplied as a co-lyophilized powder.
common_use_context: Used in research/community settings as a combined growth-hormone secretagogue; the two components act on complementary pathways.
mechanism_brief: The GHRH analog binds pituitary GHRH receptors while Ipamorelin agonizes the ghrelin/GHS receptor; combining the two is thought to produce a larger, more synergistic pulse of growth hormone than either alone.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a co-lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compounds.

name: BPC-157 / TB-500
what_it_is: A community blend combining the pentadecapeptide BPC-157 with the thymosin beta-4 fragment TB-500, supplied as a co-lyophilized powder.
common_use_context: Used in research/community settings, often combined for soft-tissue recovery contexts based on the preclinical literature on each component.
mechanism_brief: BPC-157 is studied for nitric-oxide-pathway and angiogenic effects; TB-500 (thymosin beta-4) binds G-actin and may influence cell migration. Human pharmacokinetics of both are poorly characterized.
reconstitution_note: Supplied as a co-lyophilized powder requiring reconstitution with bacteriostatic water prior to use.
fda_approved_for: Not FDA-approved; research compounds.
